title: Incorrect handling of xor with identical inputs in extract_reduce
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
test.v:
```
module test (A, B, C, D, Y);
  input A, B, C, D;
  output Y;
  assign Y = A^B^C^D^A;
endmodule
```

execute in yosys:
```
read_verilog test.v
hierarchy -check -top test
copy test test_orig
rename test test_extract
techmap test_extract
extract_reduce test_extract
techmap test_extract
miter -equiv -make_assert -make_outputs test_orig test_extract miter
flatten miter
sat -verify -prove-asserts -show-inputs -show-outputs miter
```

## Expected behavior

Y driven by $reduce_xor cell with inputs B, C, D (because A^A cancels out). Formal equivalence check passed.

## Actual behavior

Y driven by $reduce_xor cell with inputs A, B, C, D. Formal equivalence check fails. Apparently, extract_reduce does not consider cases of xor trees with multiple identical inputs.

Also, I currently don't succeed in optimizing away such cases using opt or freduce. This only succeeds in simple cases like one $xor cell driven by two identical signals. For example, opt will optimize away the double A in `assign Y = A^A^B^C^D` but not in `assign Y = A^B^A^C^D`. The latter case is successfully optimized by freduce, but `assign Y = A^B^C^A^D` and `assign Y = A^B^C^D^A` are not.
code_blocks:
read_verilog test.v
hierarchy -check -top test
copy test test_orig
rename test test_extract
techmap test_extract
extract_reduce test_extract
techmap test_extract
miter -equiv -make_assert -make_outputs test_orig test_extract miter
flatten miter
sat -verify -prove-asserts -show-inputs -show-outputs miter
